source.title: The contextual brain: implications for fear conditioning, extinction and psychopathology

confidence_rationale: Supplies the neural circuit (hippocampal configural context -> amygdala/vmPFC gate) that decides whether an inactive trace re-expresses, and shows gate failure IS the mechanism of intrusion/relapse psychopathology (PTSD, addiction, schizophrenia). Close to a direct biological endorsement of recoverability-gated return with rumination/intrusion as the failure mode. Held below 0.85 by the spatial/configural-context homology caveat -- a thin internal context tag is not homologous to the hippocampal configural representation.
failure_signatures: PTSD: failure of hippocampal/vmPFC contextual gating renews the extinguished trace in safe contexts -- intrusive re-experiencing/flashbacks are the inactive trace re-expressing as if the current context were the original.
Exaggerated dorsal-ACC + deficient vmPFC during extinction recall = the recoverability gate stuck open, the suppressed trace cannot stay suppressed.
Addiction: loss of context-specific tolerance/sensitization; drug-associated context cues reinstate seeking.
Schizophrenia: degraded context processing yields retrieval/failure-to-suppress unconstrained by situational appropriateness (intrusive thoughts, paranoid ideation).

mapping.source_claim_statement: A hippocampus-amygdala-vmPFC circuit gates whether an extinguished (inactive) trace re-expresses: the hippocampus encodes the configural context and acts as occasion-setter selecting fear vs safety memory; renewal is hippocampus-dependent; vmPFC/anterior hippocampus track extinction retrieval while amygdala/posterior hippocampus track renewal. Gate failure produces inappropriate context-independent renewal -- the mechanism of PTSD intrusion, addiction relapse, and schizophrenic intrusion.
mapping.ree_translation: Gives MECH-292's recoverability term and MECH-293's re-probe gate a concrete circuit identity: context-keyed selection between competing active/inactive traces, with an explicit prefrontal inhibitory limb. Validates ARC-060's discrete preserved-trace bank by showing return is gated, not free. Maps the rumination/intrusion failure mode onto a specific gate-stuck-open lesion.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The gating power comes from a HIGH-DIMENSIONAL hippocampal configural/spatial representation with consolidation dynamics; the review explicitly flags that even human fMRI struggles to instantiate true context. A recoverability gate keyed on a thin internal context tag is not homologous and will misfire (over- or under-renewing) exactly as impoverished human paradigms did -- the caveat is load-bearing for design, not a footnote.
mapping.source_context: Review integrating rodent single-unit/lesion fear-conditioning-extinction work with human fMRI and clinical PTSD/addiction/schizophrenia populations.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality high (Nature Reviews Neuroscience, multi-method convergence). Mapping fidelity moderate-high: a context-keyed gate with a prefrontal inhibitory limb maps cleanly onto recoverability + a regulable re-probe gate. Transfer risk moderate: configural-context homology is weak for a low-dimensional internal tag.
